data.title: Nifty holds 25,000, Sensex trades flat as broader markets outperform; defence stocks extend rally
data.description:
Defence, realty, and media stocks led the gains in trade.

While bears attempted to drag the benchmark indices, bulls managed to keep the Nifty 50 index above the psychological 25,000 mark in a lacklustre session on Friday, May 16, while the 30-pack Sensex also flitted in a narrow range.

At 3.05 pm, the Nifty 50 was quoting 25,023.05, lower by 39.05 points or 0.16 percent, while the Sensex hovered around 82,363.01, down 167.73 points or 0.20 percent in trade.

While the frontline indices traded flat, the broader markets outshone. The Nifty Midcap 100 index rallied one percent, while its smallcap counterpart soared nearly two percent, despite a muted market sentiment.

On the sectoral front, the realty and media indices shone bright, rallying 1.6 percent, while the IT and pharma gauges traded with cuts. Volatility in the market cooled, with the key India VIX gauge slipped 2.5 percent to hover near the 16.48 mark.

Follow our market blog to catch all the live updates

In the afternoon, defence sector stocks rose sharply for the sixth consecutive session on Friday, with Cochin Shipyard, Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders and Bharat Electronics Ltd (BEL) among the top gainers, as investors cheered the government’s renewed thrust on indigenous defence manufacturing. The Nifty India Defence index hit a fresh 52-week high during the session today, rising to 20 percent since May 9.

Further, global funds are pouring money back into India, driving billion-dollar corporate financing deals and sending stocks prices to near a seven-month high, as investors bet that Asia’s third-largest economy can emerge as a winner in President Donald Trump’s trade war.

Global fund managers, including those from Franklin Templeton and Federated Hermes, have quickly turned bullish, with Indian equities emerging as the top pick among Asian fund managers, a recent BofA Securities survey showed.

After offloading over $25 billion worth of Indian stocks between October and February, investors have poured more than $2.5 billion this quarter, shrugging off trade war jitters and India-Pakistan tensions. The Nifty, which was near multi-month lows on April 7, now trades within 5 percent of its September peak.

Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by investment experts on Moneycontrol.com are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol.com advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.
data.author: MoneyControl

The movement in Pidilite Industries shares has drawn attention, particularly given its inclusion in key indices such as the NIFTY NEXT 50 and NIFTY 100. The neutral sentiment analysis from Moneycontrol as of May 9, 2025, suggests a stable outlook on the stock.

Financial Performance


A review of Pidilite Industries' financial performance offers insights into its revenue, net profit, and EPS trends.

Quarterly Performance:

  • March 2025: Revenue was Rs 3,141.14 Cr, Net Profit was Rs 428.32 Cr, and EPS was Rs 8.30.
  • December 2024: Revenue was Rs 3,368.91 Cr, Net Profit was Rs 557.53 Cr, and EPS was Rs 10.86.
  • September 2024: Revenue was Rs 3,234.91 Cr, Net Profit was Rs 541.52 Cr, and EPS was Rs 10.51.
  • June 2024: Revenue was Rs 3,395.35 Cr, Net Profit was Rs 572.08 Cr, and EPS was Rs 11.15.
  • March 2024: Revenue was Rs 2,901.85 Cr, Net Profit was Rs 306.27 Cr, and EPS was Rs 5.91.

Annual Performance:
  • March 2025: Revenue was Rs 13,140.31 Cr, Net Profit was Rs 2,099.45 Cr, and EPS was Rs 40.82.
  • March 2024: Revenue was Rs 12,382.99 Cr, Net Profit was Rs 1,751.47 Cr, and EPS was Rs 34.01.
  • March 2023: Revenue was Rs 11,799.10 Cr, Net Profit was Rs 1,282.23 Cr, and EPS was Rs 25.05.
  • March 2022: Revenue was Rs 9,920.96 Cr, Net Profit was Rs 1,194.88 Cr, and EPS was Rs 23.76.
  • March 2021: Revenue was Rs 7,292.71 Cr, Net Profit was Rs 1,122.15 Cr, and EPS was Rs 22.26.

Balance Sheet


The company's balance sheet provides further insights into its financial health:
  • Share Capital: Consistently at Rs 50 Cr from March 2021 to March 2025.
  • Reserves & Surplus: Increased from Rs 5,516 Cr in March 2021 to Rs 9,703 Cr in March 2025.
  • Total Liabilities: Increased from Rs 8,830 Cr in March 2021 to Rs 14,011 Cr in March 2025.
  • Total Assets: Increased from Rs 8,830 Cr in March 2021 to Rs 14,011 Cr in March 2025.

Key Financial Ratios


Key financial ratios provide insights into the company's efficiency, profitability, and solvency:
  • Basic EPS: Increased from Rs 22.26 in March 2021 to Rs 40.82 in March 2025.
  • Book Value per Share: Increased from Rs 114.78 in March 2021 to Rs 191.79 in March 2025.
  • ROE: Increased from 20.22% in March 2021 to 21.28% in March 2025.
  • Debt to Equity: Maintained at a low level of 0.02x in March 2025.
  • P/E Ratio: 69.80 in March 2025.
  • P/B Ratio: 14.86 in March 2025.

Pidilite Industries' stock movement reflects a combination of market dynamics and company-specific factors, with financial performance remaining robust.
